quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 4
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A rescue helicopter hovers statically above a mountain ledge and drops a survival package vertically downward. If the package takes exactly $3.40\text{ s}$ to strike the valley floor below, calculate the vertical height from which it was released. (Ignore air resistance and use $g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)
A materials scientist measures the cross-sectional area of a thin alloy strip to be $0.420\text{ cm}^2$. Convert this area metric directly into square millimeters ($\text{mm}^2$).

A gardener pushes a lawnmower across a flat lawn at constant speed by applying a steady force directed downward along the handle at $40.0^{\circ}$ relative to the ground. Analyze the nature of the work done on the lawnmower by the vertical component of this applied force as it moves horizontally.
questions.explanation: The vertical component acts downward while displacement is horizontal. Force and displacement are perpendicular, so $W = 0$.

questions.question: A heavy steel demolition ball with a mass of $380\text{ kg}$ is hoisted up by a crane until it rests at a vertical height of $14.0\text{ m}$ above the target structure's foundation. Determine the gravitational potential energy stored in the ball relative to that foundation level. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

A $0.00550\text{ kg}$ bullet is accelerated from rest to a muzzle velocity of $410\text{ m/s}$ as it travels down the $0.620\text{ m}$ long barrel of a rifle. Using the work-energy theorem, calculate the magnitude of the average net force exerted on the bullet by the expanding gases.

A $1,200\text{ kg}$ compact vehicle is traveling at $22.5\text{ m/s}$ along a straight test lane. The driver applies the brakes for $4.20\text{ s}$, dropping the vehicle's momentum to $1.32 \times 10^4\text{ kg m/s}$. Find the magnitude of the average net braking force.

A mechanical conveyor system lifts $45.0\text{ kg}$ crates of produce from a loading dock up to a processing floor located $5.20\text{ m}$ vertically above it. The system transfers exactly $8$ crates every minute at a uniform speed. Determine the average useful power output sustained by the conveyor. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

An industrial electric motor draws $6.40\text{ kW}$ of electrical power from its main line supply. Internal friction and electrical losses cause the motor to dissipate $1.60\text{ kW}$ of power as waste heat. Calculate the net operating efficiency percentage of this machine.

A $0.350\text{ kg}$ laboratory cart glides smoothly along a linear track at $2.40\text{ m/s}$ and strikes a second cart of mass $0.550\text{ kg}$ that is initially stationary. The two carts couple together during the impact and move off as a single unit. Calculate their final shared speed.

A rigging system utilizes a multiple-pulley block and tackle with $4$ supporting rope segments. A worker applies an effort force of $230\text{ N}$ to lift a load of $780\text{ N}$. Determine the efficiency of this pulley system.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on work-energy theorem, impulse-momentum, and pulley efficiency.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
